conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information about Alex. I'll need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

Are you responsible for Alex? By this, I mean do you live with him and are you responsible for his care and upbringing (such as providing food, clothing, and making decisions about his education and welfare)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm fully responsible for him. I take care of all his needs - his meals, clothes, schooling, everything really. He's with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that. 

Next question: Do you normally live in the UK? And is Alex also living in the UK with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. We're based in Manchester, so no issues there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask: Is Alex under 16 years old, or if he's 16 or over, is he in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's only 8 years old, so yes, he's under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions:

Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else claiming to be responsible for him (for example, another parent or guardian)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him and I haven't claimed it yet myself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. One final question:

Is Alex currently in local authority care, or has he been in hospital or care for more than 12 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. He's just at home with me, healthy and well. He's not in any kind of care or hospital.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the requirements:

✓ Alex is under 16 years old (he's 8)
✓ You are responsible for Alex - he lives with you and you provide for his care
✓ You both live in the UK
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
✓ Alex is not in local authority care or hospital

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply, which could mean you have to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through your tax return. However, you would still be eligible to claim it.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 16 years old (he is 8 years old). 2) The claimant is responsible for Alex - they live together and the claimant provides for all his care needs including food, clothing, and education decisions. 3) Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK (Manchester). 4) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. 5) Alex is not in local authority care or hospital/care for an extended period - he lives at home and is healthy. All requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they are responsible for Alex who lives with them in the UK, Alex is under 16, no one else is claiming for him, and he is not in care or hospital.
